description: Improve Error Handling and Type Safety - eliminate any types, add a structured RespanApiError class, and handle non-JSON error responses gracefully

userStories.title: Add structured RespanApiError class
userStories.description: As a developer, I want API errors to carry structured data (status code, endpoint, response body) so callers can handle them programmatically.
acceptanceCriteria: New exported class RespanApiError extends Error in lib/shared/client.ts
Class has properties: statusCode (number), endpoint (string), responseBody (unknown)
respanRequest throws RespanApiError instead of generic Error on non-OK responses
When response.json() fails on an error response, responseBody is set to the raw text (or a fallback string) and the error is still thrown — not silently swallowed
userStories.notes: Added RespanApiError class with statusCode, endpoint, responseBody properties. respanRequest now throws RespanApiError. Non-JSON error responses captured as raw text.

userStories.title: Type query param objects in tool modules
userStories.description: As a developer, I want typed query parameter objects instead of Record<string, any> so that typos and wrong types are caught at compile time.
acceptanceCriteria: Define an interface or type alias for query params in each tool file that uses Record<string, any> for queryParams: logs.ts, traces.ts, users.ts (prompts.ts uses inline object literals and can remain as-is)
All Record<string, any> usages for queryParams local variables are replaced with the typed interface
userStories.notes: Added typed interfaces ListLogsQueryParams, ListTracesQueryParams, GetTraceQueryParams, ListCustomersQueryParams, GetCustomerQueryParams replacing all Record<string, any> usages in logs.ts, traces.ts, users.ts.

userStories.title: Remove as any casts in mcp-handler.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want proper typing on the transport handler call so TypeScript catches type mismatches instead of silently accepting anything.
acceptanceCriteria: In lib/shared/mcp-handler.ts lines 90-91, replace req as any and res as any with a typed approach
Acceptable solutions: import the expected types from the MCP SDK or Node http module and cast to those, or add a single well-commented type assertion explaining why it is safe
The word 'any' does not appear in mcp-handler.ts (unless inside a comment)
userStories.notes: Replaced req as any and res as any with IncomingMessage and ServerResponse imports from node:http. No 'any' remains in mcp-handler.ts.
